title: SACRAMENTO – Senator Becker’s portfolio of climate action, social justice, economic equity, affordable housing and patient care bills now heads to the full Assembly for the final s

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 1203 requires state agencies to publish greenhouse gas emission inventories and create plans for achieving net zero emissions from their own operations.",
    "The California state government aims to lead by example in achieving net zero emissions in advance of the net zero target for the whole economy.",
    "SB 1158 improves the way retail electricity suppliers report critical information, providing California with a more accurate picture of when the grid runs on clean energy versus fossil fuels.",
    "The measurement required by SB 1158 would help California manage its progress toward clean energy goals.",
    "SB 887 helps accelerate the construction of transmission needed to deliver new clean energy resources aimed at achieving zero-carbon electricity by 2045.",
    "SB 1112 supports equitable financing for climate-beneficial building upgrades, enabling utilities to offer inclusive utility investment programs.",
    "The Keep Families Connected Act, SB 1008, eliminates telecom fees for communications with incarcerated individuals, thereby reducing debt among families of color and households headed by women.",
    "Research indicates that telecom costs for maintaining contact with incarcerated individuals have driven more than 1 in 3 families into debt.",
    "SB 1223 facilitates greater use of the option for mental health diversion in appropriate cases, potentially increasing mental health support for Californians.",
    "SB 1407 aims to create a hub within the Governor's Office to support employee ownership in small businesses, addressing succession planning and economic instability.",
    "Transitioning to employee ownership is proven to build prosperity for all stakeholders involved.",
    "SB 948 shifts the responsibility for a transition reserve to a pooled reserve, thereby freeing up resources for affordable housing projects in California.",
    "SB 1346 establishes a pilot program to distribute unopened medications to patients in financial need, in response to the waste of billions of dollars in unused medications annually.",
    "The pilot program authorized by SB 1346 aims to reduce surplus medication waste and is intended for replication in other counties."
  ]
}
```
